is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study is entirely on Plasmodium falciparum (pfmdr1/pfcrt transfected strains).

reports_target_markers.evidence: Focus is on pfmdr1 codons 1034, 1042, 1246 (and Y184F) — none of which are the curated target positions. The only curated marker discussed (pfmdr1 86, pfcrt 76) appears only in cited literature, not as data generated here; the study strains are all N86 wild-type.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: This is an in vitro fitness/competition experiment using transfected lab strains; it reports fitness costs and merozoite counts, not allele frequencies in field populations.

sub_country_location.evidence: No field collection sites — laboratory transfected strains (D10, 7G8) obtained from WEHI, Melbourne.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No field sample collection window; lab culture experiment.

african_field_samples.evidence: Uses laboratory transfected strains (D10, 7G8), not African human field isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical in vitro study with original competition experiments.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Lab-strain in vitro study; no field dataset overlap.

supplement.evidence: No field frequency data exist in this paper regardless of supplement.

assessment.summary: An in vitro study measuring the fitness cost of pfmdr1 chloroquine-resistance mutations (codons 1034, 1042, 1246) using transfected laboratory P. falciparum strains (D10, 7G8). It is not a field survey, uses no African clinical isolates, reports no target-marker allele frequencies, and provides no geographic or temporal sampling data.
exclusion_reasons: No curated target drug-resistance codon frequencies reported (focuses on pfmdr1 1034/1042/1246)
Uses laboratory transfected strains, not African field/clinical isolates
